model_narrative: We plan production and distribution for a set of steel plants that serve several markets. At each plant we decide how intensively to run each production process, how much raw material to purchase domestically, and how much of each commodity to ship to markets, to export, or to import into a market. Running processes turns raw materials and intermediate products into final products, and the plants are limited by the capacity of their productive units. Costs accumulate from buying raw materials, transporting goods, and importing, while exporting earns revenue. The objective is to minimize the total cost across the whole system.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the steel plants where production takes place

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the market locations where steel demand must be met

sets.name: c
sets.doc: all commodities handled by the model, spanning raw materials, intermediate products, and final products

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: the final products, a subset of the commodities; these are the goods that are shipped to markets, exported, or imported

sets.name: ci
sets.doc: the intermediate products, a subset of the commodities; these are produced and consumed internally and are neither purchased nor traded

sets.name: cr
sets.doc: the raw materials, a subset of the commodities; these can be purchased domestically to feed the processes

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the production processes that can be run at each plant

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the productive units (equipment types) at the plants whose capacity limits production

params.name: a
params.doc: the input-output coefficient of each commodity in each process, in units of commodity per unit of process level; positive entries are outputs produced by the process and negative entries are inputs consumed by it

params.name: b
params.doc: the amount of a productive unit's capacity consumed per unit of process level when running that process

params.name: k
params.doc: the available capacity of each productive unit at each plant, in million tons per year

params.name: d
params.doc: the demand for each commodity at each market, in million tons per year; only final products carry positive demand

params.name: muf
params.doc: the unit transport cost for shipping final products from a plant to a market, in US dollars per ton

params.name: muv
params.doc: the unit transport cost associated with imports arriving at a market, in US dollars per ton

params.name: mue
params.doc: the unit transport cost associated with exports leaving a plant, in US dollars per ton

params.name: pd
params.doc: the domestic purchase price of each commodity, in US dollars per unit

params.name: pv
params.doc: the import price of each commodity, in US dollars per unit

params.name: pe
params.doc: the export price of each commodity, in US dollars per unit

params.name: eb
params.doc: the overall upper bound on exports of a final product, in million tons per year

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the level at which each process is run at each plant, in million tons per year

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity of a commodity shipped from a plant to a market, in million tons per year

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the quantity of a commodity purchased domestically at a plant, in million units per year

vars.name: v
vars.doc: the quantity of a commodity imported at a market, in million tons per year

vars.name: e
vars.doc: the quantity of a commodity exported from a plant, in million tons per year

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total cost over the whole system, in million US dollars

vars.name: phipsi
vars.doc: the total raw material purchase cost, in million US dollars

vars.name: philam
vars.doc: the total transport cost, in million US dollars

vars.name: phipi
vars.doc: the total import cost, in million US dollars

vars.name: phieps
vars.doc: the total export revenue, in million US dollars

constraints.name: a[cf,
constraints.intent: At each plant the total amount of each final product produced by running its processes must be at least as large as everything that final product is needed for at that plant, which is what gets shipped out to the markets plus what is exported.

constraints.name: a[ci,
constraints.intent: At each plant the net amount of each intermediate product made by the processes must be nonnegative, because intermediate products are neither purchased nor traded and so can only be supplied by production.

constraints.name: a[cr,
constraints.intent: At each plant the net amount of each raw material, counting what the processes consume together with what is purchased domestically, must be nonnegative, so that domestic purchases cover whatever the processes draw down.

constraints.name: b[m,
constraints.intent: At each plant the capacity used on each productive unit by the running processes must not exceed the capacity available for that unit at that plant.

constraints.name: x[cf,
constraints.intent: At each market the demand for each final product must be met by the total shipped in from all the plants together with what is imported at that market.

constraints.name: e[cf,
constraints.intent: The total amount of each final product exported across all plants must not exceed the overall export limit.

constraints.name: phipsi
constraints.intent: Set the raw material cost equal to the total cost of all domestic purchases, valuing each purchased quantity of raw material at its domestic price and summing over all raw materials and plants.

constraints.name: philam
constraints.intent: Set the transport cost equal to the cost of moving final products from the plants to the markets, plus the transport cost associated with imports arriving at the markets, plus the transport cost associated with exports leaving the plants.

constraints.name: phipi
constraints.intent: Set the import cost equal to the total value of all imports, valuing each imported quantity of a final product at its import price and summing over all final products and markets.

constraints.name: phieps
constraints.intent: Set the export revenue equal to the total value of all exports, valuing each exported quantity of a final product at its export price and summing over all final products and plants.

constraints.name: phi
constraints.intent: Define the total cost as the raw material cost plus the transport cost plus the import cost, minus the export revenue.

query: The board is floating a hard ceiling on exports — no more than 0.30 mill tons of steel shipped abroad in total. Plug that in and tell me where total cost lands.
expected_pyomo: model.export_ceiling = Constraint(expr=sum(model.e['steel', i] for i in model.i) <= 0.30)

query: There's a proposal on the table to lean into the export market — commit to at least 0.85 mill tons of steel exports. What's the cost picture if we do that?
expected_pyomo: model.export_floor = Constraint(expr=sum(model.e['steel', i] for i in model.i) >= 0.85)

query: Logistics wants to test throttling sicartsa's outbound — limit everything shipped out of sicartsa to all markets combined to 0.6 mill tons. What does that cost us?
expected_pyomo: model.sicartsa_outbound = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['steel', 'sicartsa', j] for j in model.j) <= 0.6)

query: Guadalajara is closest to sicartsa, so intuitively sicartsa should just supply that whole market. Couldn't we pin guadalajara's steel to come from sicartsa? What's pulling the plan another way?

query: We seem to be lighting up the old open-hearth shops a lot. Shouldn't total open-hearth steelmaking across the mills stay under 1.5 mill tons? What's keeping the model from backing off them?
expected_pyomo: model.openhearth_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.z['steel-oh', i] for i in model.i) <= 1.5)
